channel.title: Better Rule Tiles
channel.description: Last 10 notes on Better Rule Tiles

item.title: Custom rule tile scripts
item.description: Experimental feature This feature was added in version 1.5.0 as a BETA feature.

item.title: Import Window
item.description: Import Window When importing a sprite sheet, a texture sliced up to multiple sprites to the |BRT Editor or the tileset editor, this window will show up.

item.title: Sprite Overrides
item.description: Sprite override settings The sprite override settings window is where you can modify the output of a sprite, where you can assign gameobjects, set collider types, animations and more.

item.title: Tiling Rules
item.description: Tiling rules Tiling rules are what makes it possible for the rule tiles to work.

item.title: ★ BRT Editor
item.description: Getting started with the BRT Editor To use the BRT editor, you first need to create a better rule tile container.

item.title: Changelog
item.description: Version 1.5.0-b8 Version 1.5.0-b7 Fixed an error where a missing sprite reference caused the sprite drawer to break the editor Import window now works when not using universal sprite settings Padding on sprite sheets is now correctly handled in the import window You can now drag and drop multiple te...

item.title: Editor tools
item.description: The editor tools are the main way of creating and editing rule tiles using the package.

item.title: Custom Properties
item.description: Custom properties Custom properties are a way to store information in your tiles, that you can later access at runtime using a script.

item.title: Preset Blocks
item.description: Preset blocks Preset blocks provide a way to easily create multi-tile structures.

item.title: Examples
item.description: Examples The package comes with a few examples to help you better understand the tool.
